name: ncplot

metadata.name: ncplot
classifiers: License :: OSI Approved :: GNU General Public License v3 (GPLv3)

documentation:
# ncplot - Easy interactive viewing of NetCDF files in Python 



[![pypi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/ncplot.svg)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/ncplot/)
[![Documentation Status](https://readthedocs.org/projects/ncplot/badge/?version=latest)](https://ncplot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/?badge=latest)


An easy to use Python (3.6 and above) package for quickly plotting the contents of NetCDF files or xarray datasets in Python or on the command line. 

This package is designed to work with [CF](https://cfconventions.org/) compliant NetCDF data.

## Installation

To install from conda:
```sh
conda install -c conda-forge ncplot

```

To install from pypi:
```sh
pip install ncplot 
```

Install the development version using using pip:
```sh
pip install git+https://github.com/pmlmodelling/ncplot.git
```



## How to use in Python



The package is made up of a simple and easy to use function: view. To visualize everything in a file:

```sh
from ncplot import view

view("example.nc")

```

or to visualize a specific variable or list of variables:

```sh
from ncplot import view

view("example.nc", vars)

```

where vars is either a string representing a variable or a list of variables.


If you want to plot xarray datasets dataarrays, first import the xarray accessor, then plot in a similar way to xarray's built in plotting method:


```sh
import ncplot.xarray
import xarray as xr

ds = xr.open_dataset("example.nc") 
ds.ncplot.view()

```




## How to use on the command line

Command line usage is as simple as:


```sh


ncplot example.nc

```












# Reference and tutorials

A full API reference, and a how-to guide are available at [readthedocs](https://ncplot.readthedocs.io/en/latest/).

functions.name: ncplot.utils.check_lon
documentation: A function to change the lon coord identified in case it cannot be represented by hvplot

functions.name: ncplot.utils.check_lat
documentation: A function to change the lat coord identified in case it cannot be represented by hvplot

functions.name: ncplot.utils.get_dims
documentation: Extract the dimensions of the netcdf file

functions.name: ncplot.utils.is_curvilinear
documentation:
Original CDO function to work out if a grid was curvilinear.
Ideally a future version of ncplot should call this when cdo is installed.

functions.name: ncplot.plot.as_raster
documentation:
Simple hack to rasterize when datashader is available.
This is to get around Python 3.11 datashader nonavailability

functions.name: ncplot.plot.get_coastline
documentation: A function to work out an appropriate coastline resolution

functions.name: ncplot.plot.change_coords
documentation: Some model output will have repeated zeros as lon/lat. This will mess up quadmesh. Replace lon/lat with indices

functions.name: ncplot.plot.is_curvilinear
documentation:
A function originally in nctoolkit, which used CDO to figure out if a grid was curvilinear. This should
be rename to something like is_quadmesh

functions.name: ncplot.plot.ctrc
documentation: A basic control to send a message to the terminal

functions.name: ncplot.plot.in_notebook
documentation:
Returns `True` if the module is running in IPython kernel,
`False` if in IPython shell or other Python shell.

functions.name: ncplot.plot.view
documentation:
Plot the contents of a NetCDF out

Parameters
-------------
x : object or str
    xarray object or file  path
vars : list or str
    Variables you want to plot. Everything will be plotted if this is not supplied
autoscale : bool
    Set to False if you do not want the colour scales to vary. Defaults to True, which sets
    scales based on minimum and maximum values.

out : str
    Name of file if you want to store output
